message.content:
Here are the canonical OWASP WSTG v4.2 URLs and the exact INPV checklist details you asked for.

---

### 1) Authentication tests (ATHN)

All are under the v4.2 “Authentication Testing” section.[9]

1. **WSTG-ATHN-01 – Testing for Credentials Transported over an Encrypted Channel**  
   `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/04-Authentication_Testing/01-Testing_for_Credentials_Transported_over_an_Encrypted_Channel`[2]

2. **WSTG-ATHN-02 – Testing for Default Credentials**  
   `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/04-Authentication_Testing/02-Testing_for_Default_Credentials`[5]

3. **WSTG-ATHN-03 – Testing for Weak Lock Out Mechanism**  
   `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/04-Authentication_Testing/03-Testing_for_Weak_Lock_Out_Mechanism`[7]

4. **WSTG-ATHN-04 – Testing for Bypassing Authentication Schema**  
   `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/04-Authentication_Testing/04-Testing_for_Bypassing_Authentication_Schema`[9]

5. **WSTG-ATHN-05 – Testing for Vulnerable Remember Password**  
   `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/04-Authentication_Testing/05-Testing_for_Vulnerable_Remember_Password`[9]

6. **WSTG-ATHN-06 – Testing for Browser Cache Weaknesses**  
   `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/04-Authentication_Testing/06-Testing_for_Browser_Cache_Weaknesses`[4]

7. **WSTG-ATHN-07 – Testing for Weak Password Policy**  
   `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/04-Authentication_Testing/07-Testing_for_Weak_Password_Policy`[9]

8. **WSTG-ATHN-08 – Testing for Weak Security Question Answer**  
   `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/04-Authentication_Testing/08-Testing_for_Weak_Security_Question_Answer`[9]

9. **WSTG-ATHN-09 – Testing for Weak Password Change or Reset Functionalities**  
   `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/04-Authentication_Testing/09-Testing_for_Weak_Password_Change_or_Reset_Functionalities`[9]

10. **WSTG-ATHN-10 – Testing for Weaker Authentication in Alternative Channel**  
    `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/04-Authentication_Testing/10-Testing_for_Weaker_Authentication_in_Alternative_Channel`[9]

*(All paths above are relative to `https://owasp.org`.)*

---

### 2) Error handling test (ERRH)

For ERRH, see the v4.2 “Error Handling” section (subdirectory 07-Error_Handling). The specific ERRH-02 page is titled “Testing for Stack Traces” in the v4.2 content.[3]

11. **WSTG-ERRH-02 – Testing for Stack Traces**  
    `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/07-Error_Handling/02-Testing_for_Stack_Traces`  
    (The name “Testing for Stack Traces” is confirmed in the v4.2 change log and structure.[3])

---

### 3) Cryptography tests (CRYP‑01 through CRYP‑04)

These are under the v4.2 “Cryptography” section (06-Cryptography).[9]

12. **WSTG-CRYP-01 – Testing for Weak Transport Layer Security**  
    `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Cryptography/01-Testing_for_Weak_Transport_Layer_Security`[9]

13. **WSTG-CRYP-02 – Testing for Padding Oracle**  
    `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Cryptography/02-Testing_for_Padding_Oracle`[9]

14. **WSTG-CRYP-03 – Testing for Sensitive Information Sent via Unencrypted Channels**  
    `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Cryptography/03-Testing_for_Sensitive_Information_Sent_via_Unencrypted_Channels`[9]

15. **WSTG-CRYP-04 – Testing for Weak Encryption**  
    `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/06-Cryptography/04-Testing_for_Weak_Encryption`[9]

---

### 4) Business Logic tests (BUSL‑01 through BUSL‑04)

These are under the v4.2 “Business Logic Testing” section (08-Business_Logic_Testing).[9]

16. **WSTG-BUSL-01 – Testing for Business Logic Data Validation**  
    `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/08-Business_Logic_Testing/01-Testing_for_Business_Logic_Data_Validation`[9]

17. **WSTG-BUSL-02 – Testing for Ability to Forge Requests**  
    `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/08-Business_Logic_Testing/02-Testing_for_Ability_to_Forge_Requests`[9]

18. **WSTG-BUSL-03 – Testing for Integrity Checks**  
    `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/08-Business_Logic_Testing/03-Testing_for_Integrity_Checks`[9]

19. **WSTG-BUSL-04 – Testing for Process Timing**  
    `/www-project-web-security-testing-guide/v42/4-Web_Application_Security_Testing/08-Business_Logic_Testing/04-Testing_for_Process_Timing`[9]

---

### 5) Official WSTG v4.2 checklist: INPV‑14, INPV‑15, Buffer Overflow

The v4.2 checklist (as published and referenced from the official WSTG repo) lists the **Data Validation Testing (INPV)** items as follows:[1][3]

- **WSTG-INPV-13 – Testing for Format String Injection**  
  The v4.2 change log explicitly shows that this test used to be “Testing for Buffer Overflow” and was renamed to “Testing for Format String Injection.”[3]  
  > `WSTG-INPV-13 | Testing for Buffer Overflow | Testing for Format String Injection`[3]

- **WSTG-INPV-14 – Testing for Incubated Vulnerability**  
  As shown in the v4.2 checklist table:  
  > `WSTG-INPV-14 Testing for Incubated Vulnerability`[1]

- **WSTG-INPV-15 – Testing for HTTP Splitting Smuggling**  
  The checklist entry is:  
  > `WSTG-INPV-15 Testing for HTTP Splitting Smuggling`[1]  
  The v4.2 change log notes the name normalization from “HTTP Splitting/Smuggling” to “HTTP Splitting Smuggling.”[3]

- **WSTG-INPV-16 – Testing for HTTP Incoming Requests**[1]

- **WSTG-INPV-17 – Testing for Host Header Injection**[1]

- **WSTG-INPV-18 – Testing for Server-side Template Injection**[1]

- **WSTG-INPV-19 – Testing for Server-Side Request Forgery** (SSRF)[3]

#### Answering your specific INPV questions

- **What is WSTG‑INPV‑14 in v4.2 (exact name)?**  
  **“Testing for Incubated Vulnerability.”**[1]

- **What is WSTG‑INPV‑15 in v4.2 (exact name)?**  
  **“Testing for HTTP Splitting Smuggling.”**[1][3]

- **Is there a separate Buffer Overflow test ID in INPV in v4.2?**  
  No. In v4.2, **there is no separate “Buffer Overflow” test** under INPV; the previous buffer overflow test was **renamed and repurposed as WSTG‑INPV‑13 “Testing for Format String Injection.”**[3]

- **Exact INPV numbering for HTTP Splitting/Smuggling, Incubated Vulnerability, Buffer Overflow:**  
  - **HTTP Splitting/Smuggling:** WSTG‑INPV‑15 – “Testing for HTTP Splitting Smuggling.”[1][3]  
  - **Incubated Vulnerability:** WSTG‑INPV‑14 – “Testing for Incubated Vulnerability.”[1]  
  - **Buffer Overflow:** No longer present as a separate test in v4.2; former buffer overflow coverage is now **WSTG‑INPV‑13 – “Testing for Format String Injection.”**[3]
